Why estimates fail

Most estimation failures are not arithmetic mistakes. They are predictable errors of psychology and visibility, and the first step toward better estimates is understanding why good engineers produce bad ones.

workflowProgress.resultPreview:
Why estimates fail

Most people hear an estimate and store a promise. Your job as a manager is to understand why the number your engineer gives you is almost always wrong in the same direction, and why treating it as a commitment makes the problem worse.

workflowProgress.resultPreview:
Why estimates fail

The first thing to teach a new manager about estimates is that they are guesses about the future made by people who want to please you. Almost every way they go wrong follows from that sentence.

Optimism bias is the largest and most reliable distortion. When an engineer pictures a task, they picture the version where it goes well: the API behaves as documented, the test suite …
